title: The effect of volumetric (3D) tactile symbols within inclusive tactile maps
abstract: Point, linear and areal elements, which are two-dimensional and of a graphic nature, are the morphological elements employed when designing tactile maps and symbols for visually impaired users. However, beyond the two-dimensional domain, there is a fourth group of elements - volumetric elements - which mapmakers do not take sufficiently into account when it comes to designing tactile maps and symbols. This study analyses the effect of including volumetric, or 3D, symbols within a tactile map in order to do so, the researchers compared two types of maps: a tactile map based on the microcapsule method (flat) and a tactile map produced by the microcapsule method enhanced with 3D volumetric symbols (mixed). They benefited from the inclusion of a greater variety of tactile attributes. In this case, a bigger contrast height. Even non-alphabetic subjects, obtained significant improvements and is produced using thermoforming, one of the most popular systems in this field, while the other includes volumetric symbols, thus highlighting the possibilities opened up by 3D printing, a new area of research. The results of the study show that including 3D symbols improves the efficiency and autonomous use of these products.
